title: Sium bracteatum
intro: Sium bracteatum, also known as water parsnip or bracted celery, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae, native to wetlands and riparian zones in North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it a potential treatment for various gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Sium bracteatum include coumarins, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic compounds, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Sium bracteatum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of tinctures and other liquid extracts.
taxonomy: Sium bracteatum is a species of plant that belongs to the family Apiaceae. It is a member of the genus Sium, which includes several species of aquatic plants. The plant is native to the Northern Hemisphere, specifically in North America and Europe. The classification of Sium bracteatum includes its kingdom as Plantae, and its phylum as Magnoliophyta. At the class level, it is classified as Magnoliopsida.
